# Rosalind Problem: Construct the Graph of a Spectrum (in Ada)

## Problem Understanding

The task is to construct a graph from a spectrum where:
- Each vertex represents a mass value
- Each directed edge represents a mass difference between consecutive peaks
- The graph should show all possible transitions between masses

## Solution Approach

I'll implement a solution that:
1. Parses the spectrum data
2. Builds edges based on mass differences
3. Creates a graph representation

## Ada Implementation

```ada
with Ada.Text_IO; use Ada.Text_IO;
with Ada.Integer_Text_IO; use Ada.Integer_Text_IO;
with Ada.Float_Text_IO; use Ada.Float_Text_IO;
with Ada.Strings.Unbounded; use Ada.Strings.Unbounded;
with Ada.Containers.Vectors;
with Ada.Containers.Ordered_Maps;

procedure Construct_The_Graph_Of_A_Spectrum is

   type Mass_Type is digits 10;
   type Mass_Vector is array (Positive range <>) of Mass_Type;
   
   package Mass_Vectors is new Ada.Containers.Vectors
     (Index_Type => Positive, Element_Type => Mass_Type);
   
   package Mass_Maps is new Ada.Containers.Ordered_Maps
     (Key_Type => Mass_Type, Element_Type => Boolean);
   
   -- Parse input spectrum
   procedure Parse_Spectrum(Spectrum_Line : Unbounded_String; 
                           Spectrum : out Mass_Vector;
                           Length : out Natural) is
      use Ada.Strings.Unbounded;
      Position : Natural := 1;
      Temp_Str : Unbounded_String;
      Start : Natural;
      End_Pos : Natural;
   begin
      Length := 0;
      while Position <= Length(Spectrum_Line) loop
         -- Skip spaces
         while Position <= Length(Spectrum_Line) and then 
               Element(Spectrum_Line, Position) = ' ' loop
            Position := Position + 1;
         end loop;
         
         if Position > Length(Spectrum_Line) then
            exit;
         end if;
         
         Start := Position;
         -- Find end of number
         while Position <= Length(Spectrum_Line) and then 
               Element(Spectrum_Line, Position) /= ' ' loop
            Position := Position + 1;
         end loop;
         
         End_Pos := Position - 1;
         Temp_Str := Slice(Spectrum_Line, Start, End_Pos);
         Length := Length + 1;
         Spectrum(Length) := Mass_Type'Value(To_String(Temp_Str));
      end loop;
   end Parse_Spectrum;

   -- Build graph edges from spectrum
   procedure Build_Graph(Spectrum : Mass_Vector; 
                        Length : Natural;
                        Edges : out Mass_Vector;
                        Edge_Count : out Natural) is
   begin
      Edge_Count := 0;
      for I in 1..Length-1 loop
         Edge_Count := Edge_Count + 1;
         Edges(Edge_Count) := Spectrum(I+1) - Spectrum(I);
      end loop;
   end Build_Graph;

   -- Print graph edges
   procedure Print_Edges(Edges : Mass_Vector; Count : Natural) is
   begin
      for I in 1..Count loop
         Put_Line(Mass_Type'Image(Edges(I)));
      end loop;
   end Print_Edges;

   -- Main execution
   Spectrum_Line : Unbounded_String := To_Unbounded_String("57 71 101 113 129 147 156 170 186 194 208 221 234 251 267 282 298 300 315 329 342 358 367 382 392 408 416 428 434 448 457 467 473 487 497 502 514 528 539 545 558 570 584 593 605 615 627 635 649 655 664 677 691 703 709 718 725 737 749 754 761 766 774 784 790 803 814 824 835 847 852 860 867 876 880 890 901 910 918 923 933 937 947 956 967
